learning_outcomes:
LLB_learning outcomes 1.  |  Major Programme:
Graduates from the LLB Programme will have
acquired the following attributes:
(1)  |  Technical legal competence (K, S) Students will acquire skills in legal analysis
and reasoning; skills in legal research; skills in oral and written
expression of legal knowledge.
(2)  |  Broad understanding of the role of law in
society (K, V) Students will acquire an awareness of the
potential and limits of the law, in the context of broader regional and
global developments.
(3)  |  The ability to think critically and
analytically (S, V) Students will acquire and improve their generic
skills of critical thinking and logical analysis.
(4)  |  Research skills (K, S) Students will acquire and improve skills of
data collection and dissemination, analysis and reasoning, and of oral and
written presentation of research findings.
(5)  |  Ability to work in English and Chinese (K, S) Students will demonstrate a high degree of competence
in the ability to work in English. They will substantially improve
their Chinese language skills in both oral and written communication.
(6)  |  Sense of public service (V) Students will develop an awareness of how
their professional and general intellectual skills and qualifications can
benefit society at large.
(7)  |  Professionalism (V) Students will possess a strong sense of
ethics, civic duty, social and professional responsibility.
K =
knowledge S = skills V =
values
study_scheme:
LLB Bachelor of Laws (LLB) Applicable to students admitted in 2025-26
Major Programme Requirement
Students are required to complete a minimum of 90 units of
courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Required Courses:  |  45
LAWS1010r, 1020r@, 1030, 1041r@, 1042@, 1100@, 2110@, 2121@, 2122@, 2131@, 2132@, 3050, 4010[a]
(capstone course), 4151@, 4152@
2.  |  Elective
Courses:  |  45
Any
LAWS elective courses at 1000 – 4000 level (of which 21 units are
currently prescribed as prerequisites for admission into the Postgraduate
Certificate in Laws (PCLL) Programme)
Streams
Students may choose to declare
the Chinese Law Stream and/or the Global Law Stream within
the LLB Programme by taking the following courses:
Chinese
Law
(a)  |  LAWS1030, 3290
(b)  |  Two courses from LAWS2201, 2220,
2310, 3440, 3461, 4311, 4371, 4488
Global Law
(a)  |  LAWS4000,
4260
(b)  |  Two courses from LAWS2200, 2202,
2204, 2205, 2260, 2330, 3210, 3230, 3400, 3450, 3453, 3462, 4270, 4280,
4290, 4340, 4350, 4420, 4480, 4481, 4486, 4487, 4489, 4490, 4491
Total:  |  90
Explanatory
Notes:
1.  |  All LAWS courses, excluding
courses labelled as r and LAWS2220, will be included in the calculation of Major
GPA for honours classification.
2.  |  Students can opt to use LAWS2201
“Chinese Law (Summer Course)” successfully completed at the Tsinghua University
to fulfill 3 units out of the 5-unit University Core Chinese
Language Requirement in which case LAWS2201 will not be counted towards
the fulfillment of elective courses requirement.
3.  |  Exemption from 7 units out of the
8-unit University Core English Language Requirement bases on the
equivalence of courses to be taken by students (LAWS1010, 1020, 1041,
1042, 1100, 2110, 2121, 2131, 4151). Students are required to take a
1-unit ELTU course to fulfill the requirement.
4.  |  In addition to Major Programme
Requirement and University Core Requirement, students are required to
take 12 units of non-law electives.
5.  |  Students who opt for the Study
Option offered by King’s College London (KCL) and have successfully
completed the Optional Modules of 120 credits offered by KCL’s Master of
Laws (LLM) Programme will be granted course and unit exemption for up to
18 units of Major Elective Courses.
6.  |  Students who opt for the Study
Option offered by the University of Southern California USC Gould School
of Law (USC) and have successfully completed 21 units of USC LLM courses
will be granted course and unit exemption for up to 18 units of Major
Elective Courses.
[a]  |  Students who opt for the Study
Option offered by King’s College London (KCL) will be required to take
LAWS4001 to substitute for LAWS4010 in order to fulfill the capstone
requirement.
@  |  Prerequisites for subsequent
admission into the PCLL Programme. A full list of PCLL prerequisites is
listed below:
LAWS1020, 1041, 1042, 1100, 2101,
2102, 2110, 2121, 2122, 2131, 2132, 3130, 3151, 3152, 4002, 4120, 4151,
4152

Requirements for admission to the
2nd degree:
1.  |  Admission to the second degree
i.e. Bachelor of Arts (Translation) is contingent upon students’ meeting
a cumulative GPA of 3.0 or above in the first degree i.e. LLB.
2nd Degree: Bachelor of Arts (Translation)
Major Programme Requirement
Students
are required to complete a minimum of 57 units of courses as follows:
Units
1.  |  Required Courses:  |  12
(a)  |  TRAN1010, 1030, 2070, 2610
(b)  |  Any 2 courses from: TRAN4510, 4520, 4900[a]  |  6
2.  |  Elective Courses:  |  39
Students are required to complete
39 units of elective courses from the following lists
of courses, including at least one course from each of Electives Cluster A and Electives Cluster B:
(a)  |  Electives Cluster A (Translation
Studies)
TRAN2120, 3080, 3720, 4070, 4210
(b)  |  Electives Cluster B
(Interpreting)
TRAN2140,
2150, 2240, 2250, 3240, 4050
(c)  |  Other electives
TRAN1120, 1130, 2020, 2050, 2110,
2130, 2840, 3170, 3180, 3270, 3280, 3620, 3800, 3810, 3820, 3830, 3844,
3845, 3850, 3860, 3870, 3880, 3890, 4800, 4840 and unselected courses
from Clusters A and B
Total:  |  57
Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  TRAN courses at 2000 and above
level will be included in the calculation of Major GPA for honours
classification.
2.  |  Translation Major students are expected to be proficient in
both English and Chinese. Students may therefore wish to consider taking
courses that emphasize writing and speaking skills as part of their free
electives, on top of the major programme requirement. Listed below are
courses for reference: Course
for writing skills in Chinese CHLL3403 Creative Writing Course
for oral expression skills in Cantonese CHLT1302 Oral Communication Skills Courses
for oral expression skills in Putonghua CLCP3703 Advanced Putonghua CLCP4713 Current Affairs and
Discussion CLCP4723 Academic Presentation and
Discussion Courses
for writing skills in English ENGE3260 Creative Writing ELTU3413 Research Writing in the
Humanities and Social Sciences Courses
for oral expression skills in English ENGE2100 Research and Oral
Reporting ELTU3502 Essentials of Public
Speaking
[a]  |  For those students who have chosen TRAN4900 Translation
Thesis, the language used in writing the thesis should be different from
the target language of their translation project for TRAN4510/4520 (e.g.
if the student writes the TRAN4900 thesis in English, s/he must take
TRAN4510 Translation Project: English-Chinese for Final Year Project).
Explanatory Note on mutual
recognition or exclusion:
1.  |  The 12-unit non-law electives can
be used to satisfy the requirements of both the first and second degrees.

Units

SOCI2101,
2103, 2106, 2116, 2203, 2208, 2216, 2218, 2219, 2220, 3001, 3002, 3102,

Concentration Area: Students may choose to declare

Research
Methods and Data Analytics At

(a)  |  Quantitative Method Cluster: At

least 3 units from SOCI3102, 3238  |  3

3202, GRMD2104, 2105, 3104, 3106
Human

least 24 units of courses from the three modules listed below:
(a)  |  Economic Security and Society Cluster (at least 6 units and a

of Law and Governance Cluster (at least 6 units and a maximum of 9

counted towards the fulfillment of 3 units in this cluster: ANTH2530,

2085, 2300, 2345, PSYC3370, SOWK4350  |  6-9

and a maximum of 9 units): Any 6-9 units from SOCI2208, 2219, 3224, 3236, 3244, 4205 A maximum of 3 units from one of the following courses can be
counted towards the fulfillment of 3 units in this cluster: ARCH3321, 3721, DSPS2301, 3310, 3790, 3803, GDRS3005, 3006, 3007,
3008, 3024, 4006, 4007, 4008, 4011, 4012, GLSD3105, GRMD2303, 2401, 2404,
2501, 3202, 3203, 3302, 3323, 3404, 4403, 4503, PSYC3630, SOWK3340,
URSP2010, 2020, 2030, 2040, 2050, 2060, 2070, 2100, 2300, 2400, 2800,
2900, 3100, 3200, 3300, 3400, 3600, 3700, 4100  |  6-9

towards the fulfillment of 3 units in any one of the clusters of the
concentration listed above: SOCI3245[a]/4211[b],
4020[c], 4202

(a)  |  Required Courses:  |  9
SOCI2106, 2203, 3227 or 3236[e]
(b)  |  Elective Courses:  |  6
At least 6 units of courses chosen from the following, with a maximum of 3 units of DSPS courses: DSPS2201,
2301, 2501, 3202, SOCI2116, 2208, 2216, 2218, 3204, 3208, 3227 or 3236[e],
3237, 3242, 3243, 3245[a]/4211[b], 4020[c], 4202,
4410/4420[d]
Society,
Sustainability and Technology
At
least 18 units of courses from the two modules listed below:  |  18
(a)  |  Environment
and Technology (at least 6 units)
Required
Course:
SOCI3239
Elective
Course(s):
At
least 3 units from SOCI3102, 3238, 3240, 4210
(b)  |  Society
and Sustainability (at least 9 units)
Required
Course(s):
At least 3 units from SOCI3002[f], 3241[f], 4204[f] Elective Courses: At
least 6 units from SOCI2116, 2208, 2216, 2218, 3002[f], 3204, 3208, 3227,
3230, 3237, 3241[f], 3245[a]/4211[b], 4020[c], 4202, 4204[f], 4410/4420[d]
Explanatory Notes:
1.  |  SOCI courses at 2000 and above
level will be included in the calculation of Major GPA for honours
classification.
[a]  |  Sociology Field Studies
(SOCI3245)
-  |  The field studies should have a
strong component matching the theme of a particular concentration in
order to fulfill the requirement of that concentration area.
-  |  If a student declares to take two
concentration areas, units earned from the course “Sociology Field
Studies” will be counted towards the fulfillment of the requirement of
one area only, subject to the course supervisor’s endorsement. The
student should indicate towards which area the units are counted when
declaring the concentrations.
-  |  If a student has taken SOCI3245
and 4211, only up to 3 units from either one course can be counted
towards the fulfillment of the concentration requirement, also subject to
the course supervisor’s endorsement.
[b]  |  Sociology Internship:
Nurturing Competency for Meeting Future Challenges (SOCI4211)
-  |  The internship organisation
and/or the internship job nature should have a strong component matching
the theme of a particular concentration in order to fulfill the
requirement of that concentration area, subject to the course
supervisor’s endorsement.
-  |  If a student
declares to take two concentration areas, units earned from the course
“Sociology Internship: Nurturing Competency for Meeting Future Challenges”
will be counted towards the fulfillment of the requirement of one area
only, subject to the course supervisor’s endorsement. The student should
indicate towards which area the units are counted when declaring the
concentrations.
-  |  If a student has taken SOCI3245
and 4211, only up to 3 units from either one course can be counted
towards the fulfillment of the concentration requirement, also subject to
the course supervisor’s endorsement.
[c]  |  Graduation Thesis II
(SOCI4020)
-  |  The Graduation Thesis should have
a strong component matching the theme of a particular concentration in
order to fulfill the requirement of that concentration area.
-  |  If a student declares to take two
concentration areas, units earned from the course “Graduation Thesis II”
will be counted towards the fulfillment of the requirement of one area
only, subject to the course supervisor’s endorsement. The student should
indicate towards which area the units are counted when declaring the
concentrations.
[d]  |  Directed Studies I/II
(SOCI4410/4420)
-  |  The Directed Studies should have
a strong component matching the theme of a particular concentration in
order to fulfill the requirement of that concentration area.
-  |  If a student has taken SOCI4410
and 4420, and both are of the same theme that can be counted towards the
fulfillment of the requirement of one concentration only, then only up to
3 units from either one course can be counted towards the fulfillment of
the concentration requirement, also subject to the course supervisor’s
endorsement.
-  |  If a student has taken SOCI4410
and 4420 with different themes, subject to the course supervisor’s
endorsement, the units earned from SOCI4410 can be counted towards the
fulfillment of the requirement of one concentration and SOCI4420 towards
another.
-  |  Units earned from a directed
studies course can only be counted towards the fulfillment of the
requirement of one concentration.
[e]  |  Students are required to take
either SOCI3227 or 3236 in order to fulfill the requirement of Required
Courses. If a student has taken both SOCI3227 and 3236, the extra 3 units
earned can be counted towards the fulfillment of the requirement of
Elective Courses.
[f]  |  Students are required to take
either SOCI3002 or 3241 or 4204 in order to fulfill the requirement of
Required Courses. If a student has taken more than one of these three
courses, the extra 3 units or 6 units earned can be counted towards the
fulfillment of the requirement of Elective Courses.
Explanatory
Note on mutual recognition or exclusion:
1.  |  The 12-unit non-law electives can
be used to satisfy the requirements of both the first and second degrees.

Minor Programme Title Law
Minor Programme Requirement (Applicable
to students admitted to the Bachelor of Business Administration (Integrated
BBA Programme) and Juris Doctor Double Degree Programme only)
Students are required to complete a
minimum of 24 units of courses, with at least 6 units at 3000 or above
level, as follows:
Units
1.  |  Required Courses:  |  18
LAWS1020, 1030, 1041, 1042, 2131, 2132
2.  |  Elective Courses:  |  6
Any two LAWS courses at 3000 or above
level
Total:  |  24
Explanatory Note:
1. 2.  |  The Minor in Law Programme is
offered only to students of the Bachelor of Business Administration
(Integrated BBA Programme) and Juris Doctor Double Degree Programme but who
subsequently opt out of the Programme. Students who have completed
LAWS1020, 1030, 1041, 1042, 2131 and 2132 will be exempted from LAWS6001,
6021 and 6023 in the Juris Doctor Programme. No more than 12 units of courses
taken to fulfill the requirements of the students’ Major Programme(s) and
other Minor Programme(s) respectively can be used to fulfill the
requirement of this Minor Programme.
